### Data analysis #2 (meeting 15.04.2025)

Exploratory data analysis of the Batch 2 of the GeoMX data.

All the figures from this step of analysis are stored in
[[Figures_15.04.25]{.underline}](https://drive.google.com/drive/u/1/folders/18syLkpMkRi7z8jdgJzztvW6WHJv5Ijrk)

**\
** -- Stromal cell proportion: \~0.25--0.7 in stromal segments,
\~0.15--0.25 in tumor segments.\
-- Fibroblast proportion: 0--0.4 in stroma, mostly 0--0.1 in tumor.\
-- Tumor cell proportion remains high across all segments (0.3--0.8 in
stroma, avg.

5--0.25 in tumor segments.\
-- Fibroblast proportion: 0--0.4 in stroma, mostly 0--0.1 in tumor.\
-- Tumor cell proportion remains high across all segments (0.3--0.8 in
stroma, avg. \~0.5; 0.65--0.9 in tumor, avg. \~0.8).\
-- T cell proportions are comparable in both compartments.\
-- Macrophages are the third most abundant cell type in stroma and
second most abundant cell type in tumor segments.

! Need to verify stromal segments in images due to unexpectedly high
tumor cell content --- assess for spillover or misclassification.

stroma segments; moderate negative correlation in stroma and moderate

compartment-specific trends.

Contrast limits adjusted a lot and gamma

S015_Post , ROI3 stroma/tumor/full ROI

Gamma to top contrast to narrow and limits to low.

Chosen for each sample.

![](media/image35.png){width="2.7395833333333335in"
height="2.4895833333333335in"}

**Contrast Narrow (0.4--0.6)**: Increases contrast in midrange but may
clip bright/dark regions.

**Contrast Wide (0.2--0.8)**: Captures more intensity range but appears
flatter.

**Gamma \< 1 (0.5)**: Brightens midtones --- highlights dim areas.

**Gamma \> 1 (1.5)**: Darkens midtones --- emphasizes bright regions.

To check whether our "favourite genes" expression is coming from
fibroblasts, the Log2FC comparison of gene expression was done. The
analysis have shown that the S100A4 and IL1R2 gene expression don't come
from fibroblasts for CAF targets, and the IL1ORA, CCR5, CXCR2,CXCR,4 and
FGFR4 gene expression signal does not come from fibroblasts for the
CAF-derived factors and receptors.

Data analysis #5 (meeting 24.06.2025)

**PDGFRB and PDGFRA:**

Some studies report that **high stromal PDGFRB** correlates with **poor
prognosis**, but findings are **inconsistent**.

Based on IHC of PDGFRB and PDGFRA in stromal and tumor compartments in
HGSC, PDGFRA and PDGFRB **doesn't have** prognostic benefit in HGSC
\[1\].

**FAP:**

**High FAP⁺ fibroblast abundance** alone **was not** associated with
shorter survival **in the whole cohort** \[2\].

**However**, when **both CD8⁺ T-cell density and FAP⁺ fibroblast levels
were high**, outcomes were **significantly worse**:

- In the **discovery cohort**:

 - High FAP + high CD8 → **shorter PFS** (11.4 vs. 18.6 months, *p*
 > = 0.01)

- In the **validation cohort**:

 - High FAP + high CD8 → **shorter OS** (31.5 vs. 76.9 months, *p*
 > = 0.02)

1) Madsen, C. V., Steffensen, K. D., Waldstrøm, M., & Jakobsen, A.
 > (2012). Immunohistochemical expression of platelet-derived growth
 > factor receptors in ovarian cancer patients with long-term
 > follow-up. *Pathology Research International, 2012*, 851432.
 > [[https://doi.org/10.1155/2012/851432]{.underline}](https://doi.org/10.1155/2012/851432)

2) Corvigno, S., Fernebro, J., Karlsson, J. S., Mezheieusky, A.,
 > Martín-Bernabé, A., Martin De La Fuente, L., \...

012/851432]{.underline}](https://doi.org/10.1155/2012/851432)

 > Martín-Bernabé, A., Martin De La Fuente, L., \... Dahlstrand, H.
 > (2025). High prevalence of FAP+ cancer-associated fibroblasts
 > predicts poor outcome in patients with high-grade serous ovarian

 > 148--155.
 > [[https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ygyno.2025.01.010]{.underline}](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ygyno.2025.01.010)

It was important to check whether the gene expression patterns coming
from fibroblasts of the target genes differ between the pre-chemo and
post-chemo treatment samples. For this, the hierarchical clustering was
conducted. Therefore, the conclusion was that there is no bias between
the pre- and post-samples.

![](media/image36.png){width="4.243410979877515in"
height="4.309218066491688in"}

![](media/image17.png){width="2.868588145231846in"
height="3.6614588801399823in"}![](media/image12.png){width="2.8871358267716536in"
height="3.649583333333333in"}

Further, I estimated whether the cell proportions correlate with gene
expression of interest. A lot of genes of interest seem to negatively
correlate with the macrophages proportion in the stroma.

For the targets on the fibroblasts the FAP and PDGFRB are negatively

erest. A lot of genes of interest seem to negatively
correlate with the macrophages proportion in the stroma.

For the targets on the fibroblasts the FAP and PDGFRB are negatively
correlated with the macrophages, the FAP/PDGFRA and PDGFRB are
positively correlated with the NK cells (deconvolution not reliable) and
S100A4 expression is correlated with the T cells proportion.

- **FAP and PDGFRB negatively correlated with macrophages\
 > ** → May indicate that **fibroblasts with high FAP/PDGFRB
 > expression suppress macrophage infiltration or differentiation**.

- **FAP/PDGFRA and PDGFRB positively correlated with NK cells\
 > ** → Possibly suggests a **fibroblast state or signaling axis**
 > that recruits or maintains NK cells --- though you note the NK
 > deconvolution is unreliable.

- **S100A4 correlated with T cells\
 > ** → Suggests **S100A4+ fibroblasts** may promote a **T
 > cell-infiltrated** tumor microenvironment --- or vice versa.

Downregulated genes in stroma:

**SPRR1A**:\
**\
**

- **Influence**: **Decreased fibroblast differentiation** and
 > **inhibited wound healing**, leading to reduced **fibroblast
 > activation** in the tumor stroma.

**CFHR3**:

- **Downregulated in TSI fibroblasts\
 > **

- **Influence**: **Reduced immune modulation**, impairing
 > **fibroblast-immune interactions**, potentially contributing to a
 > more **immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment**.

**SOSTDC1**:

- **Influence**: **Loss of ECM regulation** and **increased
 > fibrosis**, leading to **altered tissue remodeling** in the tumor
 > stroma.

**FCHO1**:**\
**

- **Influence**: **Disruption of fibroblast-tumor cell interactions**
 > and **cell trafficking**, affecting **tumor progression** and
 > **fibroblast signaling** at the tumor-stroma interface.

**PLK1**:**\
**

- **Influence**: **Impaired fibroblast proliferation** and **cell

affecting **tumor progression** and
 > **fibroblast signaling** at the tumor-stroma interface.

**PLK1**:**\
**

- **Influence**: **Impaired fibroblast proliferation** and **cell
 > cycle regulation**, potentially reducing **fibroblast activation**
 > and **myofibroblast differentiation**.

**SYT17**:**\
**

- **Influence**: **Decreased fibroblast signaling** and **cellular
 > communication**, which may affect **fibroblast interaction** with
 > tumor cells and contribute to **reduced fibrosis**.

**SLC26A9**:**\
**

- **Influence**: **Altered ion transport**, affecting **fibroblast
 > function** in tissues involved in **fibrotic remodeling**.

**ADCYAP1**:**\
**

- **Influence**: **Disrupted fibroblast signaling** and
 > **activation**, which could impair **fibrosis** and
 > **fibroblast-mediated tissue repair**.

**TDRD12**:**\
**

- **Influence**: **Impaired RNA processing** and **fibroblast gene
 > expression**, potentially affecting **fibroblast function** in the
 > tumor stroma.

19.01.2025

Main results from the last months.

The fibroblasts located at the TSI display the myCAF phenotype. They are
enriched in matrix synthesizing pathways and this enrichment and
phenotype is probably guided by the TGF-response.

The qualities are retained both in PDS and IDS samples.

![](media/image38.png){width="6.5in"
height="7.333333333333333in"}![](media/image65.png){width="6.5in"

the TGF-response.

The qualities are retained both in PDS and IDS samples.

![](media/image38.png){width="6.5in"
height="7.333333333333333in"}![](media/image65.png){width="6.5in"
height="6.166666666666667in"}

### **Top 30 GSEA Results (PDS + IDS Samples, GeoMx Batches 1--3)**

Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) was performed using **deconvoluted
fibroblast gene expression** from PDS and IDS samples across **GeoMx
batches 1--3**. The **C2 gene set collection** (entire pathway set) was
used for pathway analysis.

The genes highlighted here are those that are **upregulated in TSI**,
exhibit the **highest fold change**, and show **significantly different
expression distributions**.

### **Spatial Immune Exclusion in TSI and Distant Stroma**

Analysis of **cell abundances** in the TSI and distant stroma segments
reveals **spatial exclusion of immune cells**, likely mediated by

une Exclusion in TSI and Distant Stroma**

Analysis of **cell abundances** in the TSI and distant stroma segments
reveals **spatial exclusion of immune cells**, likely mediated by
**myCAFs** present in the TSI. Specifically, the proportions of **B
cells, NK cells, dendritic cells, CD8⁺ and CD4⁺ T cells, and endothelial
cells** are significantly reduced in the TSI.

This analysis and corresponding plot were performed **for IDS samples
only**.

![](media/image68.png){width="6.5in" height="4.333333333333333in"}

**TROP2 in TME**

The following analysis was performed on both PDS and IDS samples.\
First, we conducted a basic exploration to determine the cellular source
of **TROP2 expression**. Our results indicate that TROP2 expression is
**restricted to tumor cells**. Specifically:

- TROP2 expression is significantly **higher in tumor AOIs** compared
 > to non-tumor regions.

- There is a **positive correlation** between the proportion of tumor
 > cells within an AOI and TROP2 expression levels.

- Additionally, **deconvolution analysis** confirms that the signal
 > attributed to tumor cells is substantially higher than that from
 > other cell types.

These findings suggest that **TROP2 is predominantly expressed by tumor
cells** within the tumor microenvironment.

![](media/image70.png){width="6.5in" height="4.333333333333333in"}

e findings suggest that **TROP2 is predominantly expressed by tumor
cells** within the tumor microenvironment.

### **TROP2 and Immune Cell Proportions in Tumor AOIs**

TROP2 expression is associated with **lower proportions of immune
cells** within tumor AOIs. Overall, the proportions of various immune
cell types are **inversely correlated** with TROP2 expression,
suggesting that tumor cells may **spatially exclude immune cells** from
the tumor microenvironment.

An important avenue for further investigation is the potential
relationship between **TROP2 expression in tumor cells** and **immune
exhaustion**, which may provide additional insight into tumor-immune
interaction
